ui_start_to_finish_status: integrated_in_app_demo_mode_available; static local launcher retained for compatibility

demo_start_path.in_app_demo_mode: open http://127.0.0.1:3000 and choose Demo Mode
demo_start_path.launcher_fallback: serve the repository locally and open /demo/final_demo_launcher.html
demo_start_path.artifact_qa: serve a temporary dependency workspace and open /demo/golden_artifacts/browser_full_runtime_qa.html

global_boundary: Fixtures prepare inputs and evidence references; they do not prove that a provider, retriever, or runtime succeeded in the current session.

demos.prompt: Create a Three.js visual with concentric audio-reactive geometry, glow, camera motion, browser-safe audio controls, compact artifact direction, and validation notes.
demos.expected_behavior: A Three.js creative-code answer and inspectable artifact with an explicit audio-start and runtime boundary.
demos.fallback_path: Use the committed Three.js artifact and browser full-runtime QA result if live generation is unavailable.
demos.success_criteria: The recorded Three.js harness renders the artifact nonblank without implying external-tool execution.
demos.evidence_note: The recorded run used a temporary QA dependency workspace rather than changing the application dependency graph.

demos.prompt: Create a p5.js generative morphogenesis sketch using flow fields, particles, branching, and emergent form with browser-safe controls.
demos.expected_behavior: A p5.js source artifact with visible structure, motion, controls, and bounded runtime guidance.
demos.fallback_path: Use the committed p5.js artifact and browser full-runtime QA result.
demos.success_criteria: The recorded p5.js harness renders a nonblank canvas without unsupported authority claims.
demos.evidence_note: The full-runtime record supersedes the older shim-only compatibility result.

demos.prompt: Design a GLSL fragment shader with time and resolution uniforms, glow, texture movement, WebGL constraints, and a static fallback.
demos.expected_behavior: An inspectable fragment shader and explicit WebGL compile/runtime assumptions.
demos.fallback_path: Use the committed GLSL artifact and WebGL QA evidence.
demos.success_criteria: The recorded harness compiles, links, draws, and observes a nonblank result.
demos.evidence_note: The result is a local WebGL render check, not a display-FPS or production performance benchmark.

demos.prompt: Create a bounded Hydra feedback pattern with oscillator layers, modulation, feedback, output routing, and explicit runtime assumptions.
demos.expected_behavior: Hydra-compatible source with a code/export boundary and a recorded hydra-synth harness result.
demos.fallback_path: Use the committed Hydra artifact and browser full-runtime QA result.
demos.success_criteria: The recorded hydra-synth harness renders nonblank without microphone or full-editor claims.
demos.evidence_note: Hydra support is limited to the named local browser harness and source artifact.

demos.prompt: Answer a creative-coding runtime question using registered official-source grounding, visible provenance, and an explicit retrieval fallback.
demos.expected_behavior: A grounded answer whose retrieved source lineage is visible separately from generated claims.
demos.fallback_path: Use the public-safe current-product evidence and canonical retrieval report if live retrieval is unavailable.
demos.success_criteria: Current-product evidence validates against its schema and excludes raw prompt, answer, reference, and excerpt text.
demos.evidence_note: Historical sanitized fixtures remain compatibility evidence rather than the current product result.

demos.prompt: Translate threshold, recursion, and return into a practical browser visual system covering geometry, motion, color, runtime, and interaction.
demos.expected_behavior: Abstract language becomes operational visual structure without spiritual or therapeutic authority claims.
demos.fallback_path: Use the committed p5.js artifact and prompt library.
demos.success_criteria: The output remains aesthetic and operational, with explicit browser constraints.
demos.evidence_note: Concept translation is creative guidance rather than cultural, religious, or psychological authority.

demos.prompt: Design a browser visual system using radial structure, recursive growth, reaction diffusion, branching, flow fields, and particles.
demos.expected_behavior: Generative-system planning with practical runtime and preview choices.
demos.fallback_path: Use the committed p5.js and GLSL artifacts and their QA records.
demos.success_criteria: The output remains implementation guidance with source and runtime boundaries.
demos.evidence_note: Recorded artifact checks do not establish broad artistic quality or production performance.

demos.prompt: Plan a browser-based installation scene with concept, geometry, audience movement, runtimes, retrieval needs, artifacts, and continuation boundaries.
demos.expected_behavior: A structured project plan and browser-oriented artifact package.
demos.fallback_path: Use the committed scenario metadata and static asset launcher.
demos.success_criteria: The output remains planning and handoff material rather than cloud deployment or external-tool execution.
demos.evidence_note: Professional creative tools remain continuation targets outside CCA execution.
